steps.prompt: Is there a finite verb in this sentence?

hints.Yes — 'Dominus' is a verb: 'Dominus' is a noun meaning 'the Lord' — it is the subject
hints.Yes — 'illuminatio' is a verb: 'illuminatio' is a noun meaning 'light/illumination' — it is a predicate nominative
hints.Yes — 'salus' is a verb: 'salus' is a noun meaning 'salvation' — it is a predicate nominative

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Dominus' would be 'Domini' — 'Dominus' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'Dominus' would be 'Dominum' — 'Dominus' is the nominative form
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'Dominus' would be 'Domine' — here 'Dominus' is the subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'Domini' ends in -i

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'illuminatio' would be 'illuminationis' — 'illuminatio' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'illuminatio' would be 'illuminationem' — 'illuminatio' is the nominative form
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'illuminatio' would be 'illuminatione' — 'illuminatio' is the nominative form

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'illuminationis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'illuminationis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'illuminationis' ends in -is

hints.Dominus: 'Dominus' is masculine — 'mea' is feminine, agreeing with feminine 'illuminatio'
hints.salus: While 'salus' is also feminine, 'mea' directly follows 'illuminatio' and modifies it
hints.et: 'et' is a conjunction — adjectives cannot modify conjunctions

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'salus' would be 'salutis' — 'salus' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'salus' would be 'salutem' — 'salus' is the nominative form
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'salus' would be 'salute' — 'salus' is the nominative form

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'salutis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'salutis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'salutis' ends in -is

translation: The Lord is my light and salvation
